Off Calendar Jan-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST. FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Julie C. Grebel-Gavery of Sheuerman Martini & Tabari (408.288.9700) for defendant and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's complaint is barred by the one-year statute of limitations set forth in Code of Civil Procedure 340.5. The statute of limitations began to run on November 19, 2008, the date of Plaintiff's written request to Defendant for all medical records, including radiology, when Plaintiff knew or should have known of Defendant's asserted wrongdoing. Moreover, Defendant shifted the burden to show that all of Plaintiff's treating physicians and radiologists were not employees or agents of Defendant during the relevant time period and that Defendant's care was reasonable, appropriate, timely and well-within the standard of care. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue on these matters. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration [Ccp 1008(A)] Of Order Granting Defts Motion For Order That The Truth Of The Matters Be Deemed Admitted; Requests For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-21-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES: 405/C. Woolard Clerk: O. Guandique

Petitioner present in pro per. Mr. Riday-White counsel present for Respondent. The Court issues a tentative ruling. The Petitioner objects to the tentative ruling. The Court find goosd cause to DENY the motion for reconsideration for the following reasons: (1) No response to the request for admissions was filed before the original hearing which resulted in the mandatory granting of the motion and sanctions, CCP 2033.280(c); (2) No new relevant facts, circumstances or law supports the motion for reconsider; (3)The motion was not brought under CCP 2033.300(b), and CCP 473 was only mentioned for the first time in the reply which is not permitted; (4) Insufficient showing of mistake, inadvertance, or excusable neglect; (5) No showing that the party who obtained the admissions will not be substantially prejudiced CCP 2033.300(b); (6) Request for additional sanctions is denied. Mr. Riday-White presents proposed order and is instructed to have Petitioner review the order prior to submission and processing. Matter not reported.
